body.title: Restructure wg_cooccur: flat table → adjacency/cluster tree
body.result: Superseded by D-wg-node-tree-architecture-2026-06-08 — architecture restated as words-as-memories (Option C). Replaced by T-wg-words-as-memories + T-wg-spread-via-cortex + T-wg-cooccur-retire.
body.proposal: Igor wants to edit wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py (HIGH inertia). Plan: . Reason: HIGH inertia write requires human approval: wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py
body.description:
Restructure wg_cooccur from flat 29M-row Postgres table to hierarchical adjacency structure with cluster tree. Current flat shape (word_a, word_b, score) requires O(N) table scans for any lookup; tree shape enables O(k) traversal from a node outward, edge pruning, and calving of cold clusters.

The word graph conceptually IS a graph (words → [(neighbor, weight), ...]) but stores as a relational table, paying scan cost at scale. Applying the same clustering/hierarchy model used for memories (with root nodes, subtrees, salience decay) to co-occurrence will unlock both performance and structural operations.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/word_graph.py (core structure), tests/test_word_graph.py (verify traversal + calving), wild_igor/igor/tools/spreading_activation.py (update propagation to use tree edges)

**Design rules:** memory shape (hierarchical tree with salience), spreading-activation (traverse edges not scan table), calving (prune weak clusters)

**Scope boundary:** IN: restructure wg_cooccur storage from flat to tree; update spreading_activation to traverse tree; add calving support. OUT: retrain word graph from corpus (separate migration ticket); change co-occurrence computation algorithm (separate tuning ticket).

**Test plan:** Unit tests for tree traversal (BFS from node); integration test for spreading_activation via tree vs. old table (verify same semantic output); calving test (prune edges below threshold, verify cluster removal).

**Gate:** This ticket is blocked — Igor must be able to reliably run his own tickets first. Bring Igor down for the operation; once he's stable, schedule this as a standalone sprint task (not automated).
